headlines.title: US stocks rise to records after a solid jobs report overshadows higher oil prices
Kevin Warsh will be the richest Fed chair ever. The Senate is ready to confirm him anyway.
Oil prices jump as US-Iran tensions intensify; Brent crude stays beyond $100
RBC revamps S&P 500 target for the rest of 2026 to 7,900
10-Year Treasury Yield Falls to 4.364% This Week

notes: Market data reflects the Friday, May 8, 2026 market close.
S&P 500 and Nasdaq set new record highs supported by strong employment data, overshadowing Middle East geopolitical tensions.
Oil prices remained elevated above $100 per barrel amid ongoing US-Iran clashes and Strait of Hormuz uncertainties.
The market is preparing for the Senate confirmation of Kevin Warsh as the next Federal Reserve Chairman, succeeding Jerome Powell when his term ends on May 15, 2026.
